summary: The audience and paid template product suggest willingness to pay for guidance, but there is not enough evidence of a repeated, painful workflow to justify SaaS yet.
recommendation: Continue validation and only test a narrow lightweight tool if recurring workflow pain becomes clear.
rationale: Niche therapist audience is real but does not alone imply software demand.
Existing paid benchmark/template product is evidence of information-product demand.
Repeated, urgent, high-friction workflow pain is not yet established.
Recurring value and recurring pricing are therefore unproven.
missing_evidence: Clear willingness to pay for ongoing usage
safe_next_steps: Interview buyers about repeated weekly or client-cycle tasks
Test a no-code or low-code guided workflow
Measure whether users return repeatedly rather than using the product once
Promote a narrow automation concept before building it
